---
title: "Visually communicate your scientific data with R"
subtitle: "Designing scientific figures"
format: 
    clean-revealjs:
        # code-fold: false
        code-overflow: wrap
        # code-block-bg: "#e60000"
        # code-block-border-left: "#31BAE9"
        code-summary: "Show code"
        highlight-style: dracula
author:
  - name: Eric Largy
    orcid: 0000-0002-6140-9788
    email: eric.largy@u-bordeaux.fr
    affiliations: 
      - ARNA, INSERM U1212, CNRS UMR 5320, Université de Bordeaux
      - UFR des Sciences Pharmaceutiques, Université de Bordeaux
date: last-modified
css: theme/custom.css
---

# Introduction to Grammar of Graphics {background-color="#184669"}

## A framework for data visualization {.smaller}

Introduced by Leland Wilkinson ([The Grammar of Graphics](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/0-387-28695-0 "Access provided by U. Bordeaux"), 2005).

Graphics can be built up from a set of components:

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Data]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: the dataset to be visualized
-   [Variables]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping of objects to values
-   [Algebra]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: operations to combine variables and specify dimensions
-   [Scales]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: represent variables on measured dimensions
-   [Statistics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: functions to change the appearance and representation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Geometry]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: creation of geometric graphs from variables
-   [Coordinates]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: mapping to coordinate systems
-   [Aesthetics]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: sensory attributes
-   [Facets]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: subplots based on subsets of data
-   [Guides]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: legends and axes to explain the graph
:::
:::::

## Adapted to R in the `ggplot2` package 

![](resources/overview_graphic-1.png)

## Example: Data 

```{r}
#| label: data
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns3070
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(DT)
library(ggplot2)

DT::datatable(mpg)
```
## Example: Data {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: ggplot_data 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mpg)
```

## Example: Mapping {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: ggplot_mapping 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)
)
```

## Example: Layers {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_layers 
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = cty,
    y = hwy
  )
) +
  geom_point()
```



## Example: Scales {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_scales
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d()
```

## Example: Facets {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_facets
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class)
```

## Example: Coordinates {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_coordinates
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50)
  )
```

## Example: Theme {auto-animate=TRUE .code5}
```{r}
#| label: ggplot_theme
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-asp: 1
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(year ~ class) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50),
    clip = "off"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    name = "Engine Displacement (L)",
    breaks = seq(0, 8, 4),
    limits = c(0, 8),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Highway Miles per Gallon") +
  labs(colour = "Vehicle Class")
```

# Reminders on variables and data types {background-color="#184669"}

## Tidyverse install verification {.out7 .code7} 

```{r}
#| label: check_environment
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

if (!requireNamespace("tidyverse", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("tidyverse")
} 

library(tidyverse)
```

## Data.table install verification {.out7 .code7} 

```{r}
#| label: check_environment_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

if (!requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("data.table")
} 

library(data.table)

# version check
packageVersion("data.table")
```


## Variables: numerics {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a <- 42

a

```

## Variables: strings {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_string <- "fourty-two"

a_string

```

## Variables: booleans {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2prime
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_boolean <- TRUE
b_boolean <- FALSE

c(a_boolean, b_boolean)
```

## Missing values and infinities {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_2second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_missing <- NA
a_nan <- NaN
a_inf <- Inf
b_inf <- -Inf
char_missing <- NA_character_
real_missing <- NA_real_
int_missing <- NA_integer_

glimpse(
  list(
    a_missing = a_missing,
    a_nan = a_nan,
    a_inf = a_inf,
    b_inf = b_inf,
    char_missing = char_missing,
    real_missing = real_missing,
    int_missing = int_missing
  )
)
```

## Vectors {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")

a_vector

```


## Vector subsetting {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")
# R is 1-indexed
# so the first element is at position 1
a_vector[c(1:3,11)]

```

## Vector subsetting by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3third
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_vector <- c(1:10, "coucou")

tibble(
  'index' = a_vector[c(1:3, 11)],
  'logical' = a_vector[
    c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, rep(FALSE, 7), TRUE)
  ],
  'negative' = a_vector[-c(4:10)]
)
```

Why are all values characters?
We will see that in a minute!

## Naming elements of a vector {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3fourth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector <- c(1:5)

names(b_vector) <- c(
  "one", "two", "three", "four", "five"
  )

b_vector
```


## Accessing named elements of a vector {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3fifth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector["three"] #named element
```


```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_3sixth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

b_vector[["three"]] #scalar element
```

## Lists {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_4
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# lists cannot contain 
# different types of data
# but can contain different types of objects, 
# including vectors

a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list
```

## List item access by name {auto-animate=TRUE} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_5
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "4|4,1,10|4,1,10,12"


a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list$mixed_vector
```

## Nested list item access by name {auto-animate=TRUE} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_6
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "7|7,5,9|7,5,9,1,10|7,5,9,1,10,12"

a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list$nested_list$character_vector
```

## Check for equality {auto-animate=TRUE} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_6second
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns7030

a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

a_list$nested_list$character_vector == a_list$character_vector
```

::: fragment

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_6third
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns8020

all(a_list$nested_list$character_vector == a_list$character_vector)
```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_6fourth
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns8020

(a_list$numeric_vector[1] + 4) == a_list$nested_list$numeric_vector[1]
```
:::

## List item access by many other ways {auto-animate=TRUE .out7} 
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_7
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_list <- list(
  numeric_vector = c(1, 2, 3, 4),
  character_vector = c("a", "b", "c"),
  mixed_vector = c(1, "b", TRUE),
  nested_list = list(
    numeric_vector = c(5, 6, 7, 8),
    character_vector = c("d", "e", "f"),
    mixed_vector = c(9, "g", FALSE)
  )
)

tibble(
  'dollar_name' = a_list$character_vector,
  'bracket_name' = a_list[["character_vector"]],
  'bracket_index' = a_list[[2]],
  'bracket_twice' = a_list[2][[1]]
)
```

What happens if we access `a_list[2]` vs. `a_list[[2]]`?

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_7p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
a_list[2]
```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_7t
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
a_list[[2]]
```
:::

## Matrices {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_8
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

matrix(1:9, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)
```
::: fragment

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_9
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

matrix(1:9, nrow = 3)
```
:::

::: fragment

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_9p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

matrix(1:8, nrow = 3)
```
:::

## Data frames are named list of vectors {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_10
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_df
```

## Names can be accessed with `names` {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

names(a_df)
```

## Vectors can be accessed with `$` {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_df$group
```

::: fragment

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11p2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
cat(
  "It is",
  is.vector(a_df$group),
  "that a_df$group is a vector, containing",
  class(a_df$group),
  "data."
)
```
:::

## Vectors can be accessed with `$` {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11t
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

unique(a_df$group)
```
::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11f
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

sort(a_df$value)
```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11fi
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

sort(a_df$value, decreasing = TRUE)
```
:::

## Dataframes can be subsetted by row/column indices {auto-animate=TRUE} 

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

# Select rows 1 and 3, all columns
a_df[c(1, 3), ]
```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# Select all rows, columns 1 and 3
a_df[, c(1, 3)]
```
:::
::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# Select rows 1 to 3, columns "id" and "value"
a_df[1:3, c("id", "value")]
```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s4
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
# Select rows where group is "A" or "B", 
# and columns 1 to 3
a_df[
  a_df$group %in% c("A", "B"),
  1:3
]
```
:::

## Dataframes can be subsetted by column names {auto-animate=TRUE} 

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s5
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_df[c("id", "value")]
```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s6
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df[["group"]] # access to scalar vector
```
:::

## Dataframes can be subsetted by logical statements {auto-animate=TRUE} 

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s7
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

# Rows where value > 10
a_df[a_df$value > 10, ]
```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s8
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# Rows where group is "A" AND value > 10
a_df[a_df$group == "A" & a_df$value > 10, ]
```
:::

## Dataframes can be subsetted with the `subset` function {auto-animate=TRUE} 

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_11s9
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns6040
a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

subset(a_df, group %in% c("A", "B") & value > 10)
```

## Use of `which` to get row indices {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

which(a_df$group == "A")

```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14_2p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

# Extract values for rows where group is "A"
a_df[which(a_df$group == "A"), ]$value

```
:::

## Use of `with` to evaluate expressions within a dataframe {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_df <- data.frame(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

# Extract values for rows where group is "A"
with(a_df, a_df[group == "A", ])

```

## Tibbles are more modern but dependent on the `tibble` package {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_12
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb <- tibble(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_tb
```

## Dataframes and tibbles can be subsetted with `dplyr` functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_13
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb <- tibble(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

filter(
  a_tb, 
  group %in% c("A", "B"), value > 10
  )
```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_13p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb |> 
  filter(group %in% c("A", "B"), value > 10)
```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_13t
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb |> 
  filter(group %in% c("A", "B")) |> 
  filter(value > 10)
```
:::

## Dataframes and tibbles can be subsetted with `dplyr` functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb <- tibble(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

select(a_df, id, value)

```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

select(a_tb, -group)  # Exclude the group column

```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_14t
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a_tb |> 
  filter(group == "A") |> 
  select(id, value)

```
:::

## The data.table alternative {auto-animate=TRUE}

Think in terms of basic units: [**rows**]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}, [**columns**]{.fg style="--col: #0c9b1f"}, and [**groups**]{.fg style="--col: #cc7504"}.

**DT[[i]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}, [j]{.fg style="--col: #0c9b1f"}, [by]{.fg style="--col: #cc7504"}]** 

  - [**i**]{.fg style="--col: #184669"}: row selection (filtering)
  - [**j**]{.fg style="--col: #0c9b1f"}: column selection (projection)
  - [**by**]{.fg style="--col: #cc7504"}: grouping operations

## The data.table alternative {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_15
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns6040

a_dt <- data.table(
  id = 1:5,
  group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C"),
  value = c(10.2, 12.5, 9.8, 11.1, 13.0)
)

a_dt

```

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_15p
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns6040

# FROM[WHERE, SELECT, GROUP BY]
# DT  [i,     j,      by]

# Select rows where group is "A"
a_dt[group == "A"]

```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_15t
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns6040

# Select columns by name
a_dt[, .(id, value)]  

```
:::

::: fragment
```{r}
#| label: base_r_var_15d
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns6040

# Select rows where group is "A" or "B", and value > 10
# Yield the corresponding id values
a_dt[
  group %in% c("A", "B") & value > 10, 
  .(id)
  ]

```
:::

# Reminders on basic operations {background-color="#184669"}

## Basic operations {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_op_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

(1 + 2) * 3 / 4 - 5 * pi
```

## Basic operations {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_op_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a <- 1
b <- pi

a + b
```

## Basic operations {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_op_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a <- 1

c <- a + pi
c
```

# Reminders on basic functions {background-color="#184669"}

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_f_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050

a <- 1
b <- pi
c <- a + b
print(
  paste(
    "The result of a + b is:",
    c
  )
)
```

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_f_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "3|4,9"

a <- 1
b <- 2
c <- sum(a, b)
cat(
  paste(
    "The result of a + b is:",
    c
  )
)
```

## Basic functions {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| label: base_r_5
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| warning: true
#| message: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns5050
#| code-line-numbers: "3"

a <- 1
b <- 2
c <- mean(c(a, b))
cat(
  paste(
    "The mean of a and b is:",
    c
  )
)
```


# Data preparation {background-color="#184669"}

## Starting dataset {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_1
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)

head(mpg)
```

## Use of glimpse {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_2
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

glimpse(mpg)
```

## Use of select  {auto-animate=TRUE .out7}
```{r}
#| label: data_preparation_3
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| classes: columns4060
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

mpg %>%
  select(displ, hwy, class, year) |> 
  glimpse()


```
